new_classes.label_en: Wine with PDO in containers ≤ 2 L with actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol (CN 2204 22 26)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.U. in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L mit tatsächlichem Alkoholgehalt ≤ 15 % vol (KN 2204 22 26)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength not exceeding 15% vol.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 22 26 are: (1) wine of fresh grapes, (2) bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, (3) presented in containers of nominal volume ≤ 2 litres, and (4) with an actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol (distinguishing it from sibling 2204 22 29 which covers > 15% vol). Criteria (1)–(3) are captured through the class hierarchy (PDOWineSmallContainerLowAlcohol subClassOf PDOWineSmallContainer subClassOf WineOfFreshGrapes), the PDOOriginQuality restriction, and the SmallContainer part restriction. Criterion (4) is partially captured via the decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent. The disjointness with PDOWineSmallContainerHighAlcohol captures the sibling discrimination. Coverage is reduced slightly because (a) the full definition of 'PDO' references Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, which is a legal cross-reference not yet formally instantiated in the TBox, and (b) the alcoholic strength threshold cannot be expressed as a full OWL equivalent class axiom without a dedicated quality-individual pattern for the numeric range — a flat datatype restriction on the class is used instead, which is an approximation.
